dialogue.utterance: Dispatch to Unit [UNIQUE_IDENTIFIER] for breathing problems at a residence.
Hi, I'm Sam, an EMT with Rescue One. What made you call 911 today?
I've been short of breath since 3 a.m. with chest pain on one side.
Tell me more about that chest pain. Is it when you breathe?
Yes, it's on the one side and feels different from my COPD.
Have you had chest pain like this before or do you think it's related to COPD?
Let me check your responsiveness. What's the time and where are we?
It's morning, around 10-something, and we're in [CITY].
You are alert and oriented to person, place, time, and situation. A&O x4.
I'm seeing a GCS of 15 with no signs of decreased responsiveness.
Your airway is patent with no signs of obstruction or difficulty maintaining it.
You're breathing quite fast-is it labored or just rapid?
Respirations are noted as 30 per minute and labored.
I'm hearing wheezing and ronchi throughout both lung fields.
Your skin is pink, warm, and dry with normal turgor. No signs of poor circulation.
Chest movement appears symmetric, and no abnormalities are seen in the neck.
You're having no trouble swallowing and no signs of neck masses or deformity.
Your breathing remains at 30 per minute, consistent with moderate respiratory distress.
Pulse is 122 and regular.
Oxygen saturation is 98% on room air.
Blood pressure is 109 over 77.
Blood glucose reading is 155 mg/dL.
When did the shortness of breath start this morning?
I awoke at 3 AM with immediate shortness of breath and pleuritic chest pain.
You said you had a recent lung surgery. Can you tell me when that occurred?
Surgery on my right lung was around March 13th, about a month ago.
We're now placing a 4-lead ECG monitor. Okay with you?
4 lead EKG monitoring successfully completed.
Let me try placing an IV. This may require a little pressure.
First attempt was unsuccessful. Let's try the right arm this time.
20-gauge IV successfully started in the right arm.
We are going to give you 0.25 mg of terbutaline to assist with your breathing.
Administered 0.25 mg terbutaline subcutaneously in the left lower quadrant.
3-11 protocol activated for respiratory distress.
Let's remeasure your pulse-it's 118 now.
Breathing has decreased to 26 per minute.
Your blood pressure is 140 over 99.
Oxygen saturation is still at 97%.
Still rating the chest pain at 4 out of 10? Any improvement sensed?
Blood glucose is now showing 0; proceeding with documentation.
A 12-lead EKG has been obtained for confirmation.
Interpreted as sinus tachycardia based on current readings.
We'll take you to [COMPANY_NAME], as you requested. Still your preference?
We're going to assist you onto the stretcher with as much help as you prefer.
Alright, I'll help you maneuver me. Don't have to pull hard.
Stretcher is now securely fastened for transport.
Subcutaneous 0.25 mg terbutaline and a 4-lead EKG were performed with a 12-lead result.
We're now en route to [COMPANY_NAME] with vitals adjusted and respiratory distress improving post-terbutaline.
Patient remains in moderate respiratory distress but is improving with medication.
Secondary interventions include 12-lead EKG and verified IV site placement.
All interventions completed on time with no issues.
Constant observation for COPD or post-operative complications advised.
Post-care planning is ongoing with hospital for assessment and follow up.
Arriving at [COMPANY_NAME] with a ready-to-deliver patient care summary.
Patient is on the stretcher, alert and oriented with no signs of disorientation.
All findings and vitals are properly recorded for hospital documentation.
Documents handed to the ED nurse with IV secured and monitoring active.
Patient is stable en route with improved breathing post-arrival.
All transport documentation is delivered to the hospital.
Patient's condition is stable with no incidents, and communication delivered consistently.
Initial EKG results shared with the ED team for processing.
Transferred patient to hospital bed under nurse supervision upon arrival.
Patient moved smoothly from stretcher to bed with staff confirmation.
Patient is cooperative during all transfers and exchanges.
Notes transferred to the ED registration and care team upon arrival.
EPCR reviewed for completeness and timestamps confirmed.
Patient ambulated to the bed with medical assistance at ED.
Final documents added and logs revised for accurate transport.
Patient improved after terbutaline and all interventions recorded for hospital review.
Patient remains alert and compliant with hospital intake procedures.
Final charting was consistent and transferred without errors.
EMS actions were confirmed and followed emergency protocols.
Moderate respiratory distress and diagnosis remain for management.
No issues noted during transport, as stated in EPCR and hospital logs.
Interventions follow procedures and EKG confirmed for hospital understanding.
Final updates on patient condition and transport are well documented for records.
Reason for call confirmed and medications were effective as per EPCR.
Post-medication report matches with patient's reported improvements and EMT feedback.
Patient continued to assist during all transfers and was conscious.
Medical care was consistent with transfer notes and ePCR findings.
Plan for hospital follow-up identified and shared upon intake.
All care and procedures followed appropriately during transfer and intake.
EMS actions are valid and within EPCR logs for accountability and evaluation.
Protocol 3-11 completed with all actions recorded accurately.
